conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

First, do you have a child or children that you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do. I have a child, Alex, who's 18 and lives with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. 

To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to understand a bit more about their situation. Since Alex is 18, their eligibility depends on whether they're in approved education or training.

Is Alex currently in full-time approved education or training (such as A-levels, Scottish Highers, or similar qualifications)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in any education or training at the moment. They finished school but aren't studying anything right now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information.

Just to confirm - is Alex under the age of 20, or are they 20 or older? (I need to confirm their exact age to make a final determination.)

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that.

Just one more question to complete my assessment: Do you live in the UK, or are you otherwise subject to UK jurisdiction for benefits purposes (for example, through working for a UK employer abroad or serving in HM Forces overseas)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on the information you've provided, **you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's why: Child Benefit can be claimed for children under 16, or for young people aged 16-19 who are in approved full-time education or training (such as A-levels, Scottish Highers, or similar qualifications). Since Alex is 18 and is not currently in any education or training, they don't meet the criteria for the extended age range.

If Alex decides to return to approved education or training in the future, you may become eligible for Child Benefit again, provided they meet the other requirements. You would need to make a new claim or notify the Child Benefit Office of the change in circumstances.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 18 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 18 years old and is not currently in full-time approved education or training. Under UK Child Benefit rules, Child Benefit can be claimed for children under 16, or for young people aged 16-19 who are in approved education or training. Since Alex has finished school and is not studying or in any training program, they do not meet the criteria for the extended age range (16-19). Therefore, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK and is responsible for their 18-year-old child, Alex, who lives with them. However, Alex is not currently in any education or training. Since Child Benefit eligibility for young people aged 16-19 requires them to be in approved education or training, and Alex does not meet this requirement, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

Therefore, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK and is responsible for their 18-year-old child, Alex, who lives with them. However, Alex is not currently in any education or training. Since Child Benefit eligibility for young people aged 16-19 requires them to be in approved education or training, and Alex does not meet this requirement, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
